channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Supreme Court blocks, for now, new deportations under 18th century wartime law
item.description: The Supreme Court on Saturday blocked, for now, the deportations of any Venezuelans held in northern Texas under an 18th century wartime law.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As DEI rollbacks take hold, students of color say they're losing campus support systems
item.description: As U.S. colleges are pulling back on diversity, equity and inclusion practices, students of color say they are starting to lose campus mentors, move-in events, scholarships and diversity offices where they always have felt welcome on predominately white campuses.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration makes major cuts to Native American boarding school research projects
item.description: At least $1.6 million in federal funds for projects meant to capture and digitize stories of the systemic abuse of generations of Indigenous children in boarding schools at the hands of the U.S. government have been slashed due to federal funding cuts under President Donald Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former President Bill Clinton returns to Oklahoma City 30 years after the bombing
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Thirty years after the deadliest homegrown attack in U.S. history, former President Bill Clinton will return to Oklahoma City on Saturday to remember the people who were killed and comfort those affected by the bombing.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Questions emerge about how a deputy's stepson became the accused gunman in deadly FSU shooting
item.description: After a deadly shooting at Florida State University, questions are emerging about how the stepson of a beloved sheriff’s deputy tasked with school safety at a middle school became the accused gunman.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne And Heather Hollingsworth, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Iran and the US prepare for a second round of negotiations over Tehran's nuclear program in Rome
item.description: ROME (AP) — Iran and the United States prepared Saturday for a second round of negotiations over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program in Rome The talks in Italy over Easter weekend again will hinge on U.S. billionaire Steve Witkoff, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump’s first quarter approval rating sits at 45 percent: Poll
item.description: Less than half of U.S. adults say they have confidence in the president to recommend or do the right thing for the economy, according to the Gallup poll.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Partisan divide widening on US support for NATO: Pew
item.description: Among party lines, Democrats' opinion about NATO being beneficial to the U.S. has largely stayed the same, but this view has dropped off by 6 points among GOP respondents.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Inside the 'rape club prison': Former inmates tell their stories
item.description: They tell NewsNation they were assaulted by officers who worked there, and when they tried to report the abuse, they were silenced
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Ford halts shipments of F-150s and other models to China: Report
item.description: "We have adjusted exports from the U.S. to China in light of the current tariffs," a Ford spokesperson told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Ten Commandments, ‘In God We Trust’ in classrooms is now Arkansas law
item.description: Arkansas Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders signed a bill into law this week making religious displays in classrooms state law.
item.dc:creator: Alex Kienlen, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Deliberations to resume Monday at bribery trial of Nadine Menendez, wife of ex-US Sen. Bob Menendez
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A jury will resume deliberations Monday in the bribery case brought against Nadine Menendez after a prosecutor told a jury that she was a “critical participant” in a five-year fraud with her husband, former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: Menendez brothers' move, Alex Murdaugh's accomplice, Luigi Mangione's charges
item.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indiana takes a first -- and long -- shot at acquiring parts of Illinois
item.description: Indiana is taking a first step toward acquiring parts of Illinois, though a last step appears rather unlikely.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Anti-Israel agitator charged in violent hate crime attack on two Jewish college students
item.description: An Illinois man is facing four felony counts stemming from an alleged hate crime attack on two Jewish students at Chicago's DePaul University last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks CFPB layoffs, probes potential violation of order
item.description: A federal judge temporarily barred the Trump administration from laying off roughly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) as she mulls whether it violates her previous order. At a Friday hearing, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ryan Gosling to star in next 'Star Wars' film, Lucasfilm announces
item.description: The Force was with many Japanese, as well as visitors from abroad, at a “Star Wars” event on Friday where Lucasfilm announced the next installation in the film franchise.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, Fox 5

item.title: Trump admin to cut 90% of CFPB in latest layoffs: Reports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is cutting nearly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) employees in a push to significantly downsize the watchdog agency formed to shield U.S. consumers from financial fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at Harvard's international students and tax-exempt status
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has escalated its ongoing battle with Harvard, threatening to block the university from enrolling international students as the president called for withdrawing Harvard’s tax-exempt status.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, FATIMA HUSSEIN and ALIA WONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about the shooting at Florida State University
item.description: Students and staff have returned to buildings on the Florida State University campus to retrieve their belongings a day after a gunman opened fire, killing two people and wounding at least six others.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supreme Court to hear rare May arguments in birthright citizenship battle
item.description: The Supreme Court scheduled a rare May oral argument on the Trump administration’s emergency request to narrow a series of nationwide blocks on President Trump’s executive order that would restrict birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with more than 1,000 fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two dead and 6 injured in Florida State shooting; gunman is deputy's son, police say
item.description: Authorities identified the suspected shooter as a 20-year-old student who is also the son of a local sheriff's deputy.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, WGN 9

item.title: Obama, Sanders, Ben Carson among most liked politicians, surveys show
item.description: Former President Barack Obama, Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) and former Secretary of Housing and Urban Development Ben Carson are among the most liked politicians, according to new YouGov surveys.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building in China, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Kamala Harris holds lead in hypothetical California governor race, poll shows
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris would enjoy a wide lead over the rest of the field if she were to enter the 2026 race for California governor, the results of a recent poll show.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, WGN 9

item.title: Republicans consider increasing taxes on the rich in break from party orthodoxy
item.description: Republicans in Congress are considering increasing taxes on the rich as a part of President Trump’s “big, beautiful bill” of ambitious legislative priorities, a striking development that breaks with decades of party orthodoxy and is spurring alarm bells from traditional conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete more than a week after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his possible role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KTLA 5

item.title: Proposed rule change on endangered species triggers alarm for environmentalists
item.description: The Trump administration plans to eliminate habitat protections for endangered and threatened species in a move environmentalists say would lead to the extinction of critically endangered species because of logging, mining, development and other activities.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Temu and Shein say they're raising prices due to tariffs
item.description: Both companies said they would be making “price adjustments” starting April 25, although neither provided details about the size of the increases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Lost toddler found miles from home by dog that guided him to safety, sheriff says
item.description: When an Arizona toddler wandered away from home Monday, a team of deputies, search and rescue volunteers and rangers began their search. In the end, it was a protective pup who track down the 2-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: California sues to stop Trump from imposing sweeping tariffs
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom sued the Trump administration on Wednesday, challenging the president's authority to impose sweeping tariffs that have set off a global trade war.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family says ICE agents smashed car window in seizing Guatemalan man who's seeking asylum
item.description: NEW BEDFORD, Mass. (AP) — A Massachusetts family is demanding answers from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, complaining its agents smashed a car window with a hammer and detained a man who they claim had applied for asylum.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Attorney: ICE agents shatter car window during arrest
item.description: Federal immigration agents reportedly shattered a car window in New Bedford while detaining a Guatemalan man on Monday—despite his attorney saying they had the wrong person.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House removes wire spot from press pool
item.description: The White House is making changes to which outlets are included in the press pool covering President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: A Georgia man was arrested after federal authorities said he made numerous death threats against Director of National Intelligence (DNI) Tulsi Gabbard, the Justice Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9
